title: Wardrobe Wednesday - Shabby Apple
excerpt: I'll be honest, I have adopted a uniform for the past several months. It consists of leggings (yes, I wear them as pants), a v-neck tee shirt, and some sort of cardigan like sweater/sweatshirt that doesn't have a zipper. It's…
body: I'll be honest, I have adopted a uniform for the past several months. It consists of leggings (yes, I wear them as pants), a v-neck tee shirt, and some sort of cardigan like sweater/sweatshirt that doesn't have a zipper. It's comfortable, easy and doesn't take much thought in the mornings. But since feeling better and getting back to my pre-pregnancy weight, I am somewhat eager to once again dress up without the worry of how I'm going to nurse in a few hours. So when Shabby Apple asked if they could send me a dress and offer a giveaway to one of my readers, I happily obliged. I chose this dress (which is no longer available), and was delightfully surprised at the fit. Naturally, I was a bit hesitant to put something so form fitting on. But my mind changed the minute I zipped it up, and I immediately wished I had somewhere to wear it (other than my backyard for this photo). I am so impressed with the company's mission to help change the lives of women around the world by sharing a percentage of profits with various organizations working to bring life-changing micro-loans to the world's working poor. Learn more here.
